guideline_references: ADS/Diabetes Australia — Management of hypoglycaemia in adults (severe hypoglycaemia / glucagon rescue)
ANZCOR Guideline 9.2 — first aid for the unconscious casualty (recovery position, nil by mouth)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who administered 1 mg IM glucagon and, once the patient could swallow, oral carbohydrate. The AI Doctor CANNOT give an IM/parenteral drug; it is scored on recognising severe neuroglycopenic hypoglycaemia, escalating to 000, instructing the partner to give the patient's own in-date glucagon rescue kit if available and trained, positioning in recovery, and giving NOTHING by mouth until consciousness and swallow return.

Known baseline condition legitimately stated by the carer (insulin-treated diabetes on a basal-bolus regimen); the NEW acute complication (severe symptomatic hypoglycaemia with altered mental status) is the sealed answer.
Aspiration trap: the classic error is to instruct oral carbohydrate / 'give him juice' while the patient is obtunded and cannot protect his airway — this is an error of commission.
Patient-obtainable bedside capillary glucose reading carried as a verbatim STRING in objective_data_offered (never parsed to a structured number, never a lab_result).
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source note EMD-021.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
